title: phaius tetragonus
intro: Phaius tetragonus, also known as the Four-angled Phaius or Four-angled Orchid, is a type of terrestrial orchid that belongs to the family Orchidaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Phaius tetragonus include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and treat wounds, making it a valuable herb in folk medicine. In terms of herbal preparations, the plant is often used in infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, and is also consumed as a raw or cooked vegetable in some parts of the world.
